name: Placement Scoring Algorithm
description: Determines level assignment from diagnostic assessment scores using majority-rules with speaking-priority tiebreaker.

algorithm.step_1: Score each of the 4 modules on a 0-100 scale (vocabulary scored as % of 5000-word ceiling)
algorithm.step_2: Map each module score to a level using the thresholds above
algorithm.step_3: Count the most frequent level across all 4 modules
algorithm.step_4: If tied: the SPEAKING score breaks the tie (strongest predictor of communicative competence)
algorithm.step_5: If any dimension is 20+ points higher than another, FLAG for human review
algorithm.step_6: Apply refinement: if 2 strongest dimensions are a full level above the majority, default to HIGHER placement on a 2-week trial

examples.result: L2 (refinement: 2 strongest at L2, trial placement at higher level)
FLAG FOR REVIEW (20+ gap between dimensions, no clear majority)

on_assignment: DM learner with level result + personalized study plan
Assign Discord role (🌱 Level 0 / 💪 Level 1 / 🚀 Level 2 / 👑 Level 3)
on_flag: Default to HIGHER of the two majority levels (on 2-week trial)
Schedule a 5-minute human placement check within 48 hours
Enable fast-track re-placement (can change level in first week)
post_placement_actions.fast_track_replacement: If a learner is clearly misplaced (bored or overwhelmed) in their first week, they can be moved immediately without waiting for an advancement exam.

implementation_notes.pilot_phase: Founder assigns levels manually based on quick assessment. Full algorithm is for Phase 2+.
implementation_notes.ai_scoring: Speaking and accent modules require AI scoring (or human evaluation). Listening, vocabulary, and grammar are auto-scored (objective answers).
implementation_notes.data_capture: All placement results are stored for calibration. When enough data exists (50+ placements), validate the algorithm against actual learner performance at their assigned level.
